sections.heading: Explainable AI (XAI)
sections.body: Explainable AI aims to make a system's decisions understandable to humans — rather than treating a model as an opaque black box, XAI techniques try to reveal which factors most influenced a given prediction or recommendation.

sections.heading: Fairness
sections.body: Fairness in AI means ensuring a system's outcomes don't systematically disadvantage particular groups — this is closely tied to the bias-in-data problem introduced in Unit 1, and remains an active area of both technical and policy research.

sections.heading: Transparency
sections.body: Transparency means being open about how an AI system works, what data it was trained on, and its known limitations — giving users and stakeholders enough information to reasonably trust (or appropriately distrust) the system's outputs.

sections.heading: Accountability
sections.body: Accountability means having clear responsibility for an AI system's outcomes — ensuring there's a person or organization answerable for how the system is built, deployed, and for correcting it when it causes harm.

sections.heading: Ethics
sections.body: AI ethics is the broader field examining the moral implications of building and deploying AI systems — covering questions of harm, consent, autonomy, and the societal impact of automating decisions that used to require human judgment.

sections.heading: Responsible AI
sections.body: Responsible AI is the practical umbrella bringing fairness, transparency, accountability, and ethics together into concrete development practices — treating these as design requirements throughout a system's lifecycle, not an afterthought bolted on at the end.

quiz.question: Explainable AI (XAI) primarily aims to:
options: Make model decisions understandable to humans
Eliminate the need for training data

quiz.question: Accountability in AI means:
options: No one is responsible for outcomes
There is clear responsibility for a system's outcomes and correcting harm
The system must be fully autonomous with no oversight
Only the end user is responsible

quiz.question: Responsible AI is best described as:
options: Bringing fairness, transparency, accountability, and ethics into concrete practices throughout a system's lifecycle
An optional afterthought with no real requirements
